steps.title: Identify the zeros from the table
steps.content:
Read the table to find where $p(x) = 0$:
  • At $x = -1$: $p(-1) = 0$
  • At $x = 2$: $p(2) = 0$

steps.title: Apply the Factor Theorem
steps.content:
The Factor Theorem states: if $p(r) = 0$, then $(x - r)$ is a factor of $p(x)$.
Since $p(-1) = 0$, we know $(x + 1)$ is a factor.
Since $p(2) = 0$, we know $(x - 2)$ is a factor.
steps.formula: $$\text{If } p(r) = 0 \text{, then } (x - r) \text{ is a factor}$$

steps.title: Match both factors to the answer choice
steps.content:
We need a choice containing both $(x + 1)$ and $(x - 2)$ as factors.
  • A: $(x - 3)$ — would require $p(3) = 0$; not in table
  • B: $(x + 3)$ — would require $p(-3) = 0$; not in table
  • C: $(x - 1) \times (x + 2)$ — would require $p(1) = 0$ and $p(-2) = 0$, but the table shows $p(1) = -1$ and $p(-2) = 5$
  • D: $(x + 1) \times (x - 2)$ — matches both our factors ✓
$(x + 1)(x - 2)$ matches choice D — D is correct.
